title: Nutanix Unified Storage Achieves NVIDIA Certification as Enterprises Race to Build AI Factories
author: Na
description: Nutanix announced that its Unified Storage (NUS) solution has achieved NVIDIA-Certified enterprise-level status, validating its capability to support large-scale production AI workloads. The certification demonstrates full-stack interoperability with NVIDIA AI infrastructure, enabling linear scalability from 10 nodes supporting 32 GPUs to configurations supporting 1,024 GPUs. Nutanix also plans to support NVIDIA BlueField-4 STX in the second half of 2026, enhancing data velocity and storage efficiency for AI operations.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Nutanix received enterprise-level NVIDIA certification for its storage solution, validating its AI infrastructure capabilities. The certification demonstrates technical achievement, reduces deployment risk for customers, and positions the company favorably in the growing AI infrastructure market. Planned BlueField-4 STX support further strengthens its AI-native storage roadmap.

NVIDIA's certification program and technology ecosystem (Spectrum-X, BlueField DPUs, GPUs) are being validated through Nutanix's enterprise deployment. This expands NVIDIA's addressable market for AI infrastructure solutions and demonstrates the maturity of its full-stack AI platform, supporting broader enterprise adoption of NVIDIA-powered AI factories.

title: NVIDIA Vera BlueField-4 STX Brings Agentic AI Storage Processing With In-Silicon Security
author: Nvidia
description: NVIDIA announced new security innovations for Vera BlueField-4 STX, introducing enhanced DOCA Vault, Argus, and Flow capabilities designed to protect agentic AI systems. The platform delivers runtime threat detection up to 1,000x faster than existing solutions while enforcing network and file access policies at speeds up to 800Gb/s. Multiple cybersecurity and storage partners are integrating with the platform, with availability expected in H2 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: NVIDIA is launching advanced security capabilities for its BlueField-4 platform with significant performance improvements (1,000x faster threat detection) and broad ecosystem adoption from major cybersecurity and storage vendors, positioning the company as a leader in secure AI infrastructure.
Akamai is integrating enterprise security solutions with Vera BlueField-4 STX, gaining access to a growing agentic AI market and expanding its security portfolio offerings.
Cisco is among the cybersecurity leaders integrating with the STX platform, positioning itself to serve enterprise AI security needs.
Palo Alto Networks is integrating with Vera BlueField-4 STX, expanding its presence in the emerging agentic AI security market.
CrowdStrike is partnering with NVIDIA on STX-based security solutions, gaining exposure to the growing agentic AI infrastructure market.
Fortinet is integrating security solutions with the BlueField-4 STX platform, positioning itself in the emerging secure AI storage ecosystem.
Dell is building STX-based AI-native storage platforms, enabling it to serve enterprise agentic AI deployments with integrated security.
HPE is developing STX-based storage platforms, positioning itself to capture demand in the secure AI storage infrastructure market.
HPE is developing STX-based storage platforms, positioning itself to capture demand in the secure AI storage infrastructure market.
NetApp is building on STX to deliver AI-native storage infrastructure, expanding its portfolio for enterprise agentic AI deployments.
IBM is among storage providers building STX-based platforms, enabling it to serve the growing agentic AI storage market.
Nutanix is developing STX-based storage platforms, positioning itself in the emerging secure AI infrastructure market.
Accenture is working as a global systems integrator to bring secure AI storage solutions to enterprises, expanding its AI infrastructure consulting services.

title: ServisFirst Gets Axed by Champlain — a Minor Position in a Major Drawdown
author: Seena Hassouna
description: Champlain Investment Partners completely exited its position in ServisFirst Bancshares, selling 1.57 million shares worth approximately $124.23 million. However, the exit appears to be part of broader fund downsizing rather than a targeted judgment on the company, as Champlain's reportable AUM contracted by roughly $2 billion quarter-over-quarter. The position represented only 1.14% of the fund's prior-quarter holdings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While Champlain completely exited the position, the article emphasizes this was a minor holding (1.14% of AUM) liquidated during broad fund contraction rather than a conviction-based exit. The stock has underperformed the market by 29 percentage points over the past year, but the filing alone provides insufficient signal to warrant negative sentiment without corroborating institutional moves.
Listed as a top holding in Champlain's portfolio at $152.57 million (1.9% of AUM) and mentioned as a stock The Motley Fool recommends, indicating institutional confidence.
Ranked as a top holding in Champlain's portfolio at $153.19 million (1.9% of AUM) and recommended by The Motley Fool.
Second-largest holding in Champlain's portfolio at $161.36 million (2.0% of AUM) and recommended by The Motley Fool.
Maintained as a top holding in Champlain's portfolio at $152.43 million (1.9% of AUM) and recommended by The Motley Fool.

title: From CrowdStrike to Chewy, These Tanking Stocks Are Announcing Buybacks
author: Leo Miller
description: CrowdStrike, Chewy, and Nutanix have all seen their stock prices decline significantly (30-50% from highs) but are announcing substantial buyback programs to signal confidence in their valuations. CrowdStrike authorized an additional $500 million buyback amid AI-driven cybersecurity concerns, Chewy tripled its buyback capacity to $750 million despite flat pet household formations, and Nutanix increased buybacks to $779 million while facing longer server lead times. These buyback announcements suggest management believes shares are undervalued despite near-term headwinds.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock down 30% from highs due to AI disruption fears in cybersecurity. Company authorized $500M additional buyback (total $1.5B capacity) signaling confidence, but authorization is relatively small. Management sees improving momentum but acknowledges near-term AI concerns unlikely to abate. Analyst consensus price target implies 30% upside.

Stock down 40% from 52-week high due to flat pet household formations limiting addressable market growth. Company tripled buyback capacity to $750M (7% of market cap), a solid confidence indicator. However, company explicitly stated it does not forecast significant rebound in household formations metric.

Stock down 50% from 52-week high. Company lowered revenue and free cash flow outlook due to longer server lead times impacting revenue timing. While Nutanix increased buyback authorization substantially to $779M (8.5% of market cap), the significant stock decline and reduced guidance indicate material near-term challenges despite management confidence.

title: IGEL Recognizes Nutanix, Omnissa, NVIDIA, HP and Imprivata as 2026 IGEL Ready Partners of the Year
author: Not Specified
description: IGEL announced the winners of its 2026 Ready Partner of the Year Awards at the IGEL Now & Next conference in Miami Beach. The awards recognized partners excelling in secure endpoint solutions and cloud workspace deployment. Winners included Nutanix (Global Partner), Omnissa (Technology Partner), HP (Accelerated Growth), Imprivata (Application Excellence), and NVIDIA (Cybersecurity Excellence).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Named IGEL Ready Global Partner of the Year 2026, recognized for strong technical alignment and helping enterprises simplify VDI and hybrid cloud deployments with reduced complexity and risk.
Won IGEL Ready Accelerated Growth Partner of the Year 2026, indicating strong partnership growth and contribution to IGEL's ecosystem expansion.
Awarded IGEL Ready Cybersecurity Excellence Partner of the Year 2026, recognized for contributions to cybersecurity innovation in the endpoint security ecosystem.
